name: Honey-Butternut Stir-Fry
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Celery, cut into diagonal slices
2 teaspoons Honey (or substitute or eliminate for those who object to honey)
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel, seed, and slice the butternut squash into 1/4-inch slices.
2. Cut the squash slices into small pie-shaped wedges (should yield about 2 cups total).
3. Heat oils in a 10-inch skillet or wok over medium heat.
4. Add squash, celery, garlic, and gingerroot to the hot oil; stir-fry for 3 minutes.
5. Add broccoli and onion; continue stir-frying for an additional 3 to 4 minutes, or until vegetables are crisp-tender.
6. In a small bowl, combine lemon juice and honey (or substitute or eliminate for those who object to honey).
7. Toss the lemon-honey mixture with the stir-fried vegetables.
8. Sprinkle sunflower nuts on top.
9. Serve immediately. Makes 5 to 6 servings.
Per serving: 127 calories, 2 grams protein, 14 grams carbohydrates, 8 grams fat, 0 milligrams cholesterol, 19 milligrams sodium, 387 milligrams potassium, 4 grams fiber, 124% of the Daily Recommended Intake (DRIs) Vitamin A, 40% DRIs Vitamin C.
